name: Higher-Kinded Types
def: Generics that abstract over type constructors themselves (F<_>), not just over concrete types.
problem: Write one interface — Functor, Monad, Traversable — that works for any container (Option, List, Future) instead of re-implementing it per type.
mechanism: A type parameter ranges over a type constructor of kind * → * (an F such that F<A> is a type), letting code be generic in the "shape" that wraps values, not just the value type.
benefits: Express Functor/Monad/Monoid generically and reuse them
failures: Reaching for HKTs where a concrete type suffices
Leaky encodings mistaken for real kind polymorphism
whenNot: Mainstream code with a single concrete container
myths: "Generics already give this" — ordinary generics abstract over types; HKTs abstract over type constructors
